summary: Partially decompiled the common fighter CaptureKirby code by adding `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKirby.c/.h`, moving several routines out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, wiring the new C object into `obj_files.mk`, and adding motion-variable/header support needed by the new code. The PR report shows the new file at 84.07% overall, with `ftCo_800BD19C` and the empty Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks fully matching, while larger routines `ftCo_800BD1DC` and `ftCo_800BD39C` remained partial.
what_changed: Added `ftCo_CaptureKirby.c` with C implementations/stubs for `ftCo_800BD19C`, `ftCo_800BD1DC`, `ftCo_CaptureKirby_Anim`, `ftCo_CaptureKirby_IASA`, `ftCo_CaptureKirby_Phys`, `ftCo_CaptureKirby_Coll`, and `ftCo_800BD39C`.
Removed the corresponding CaptureKirby assembly block from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`; only nearby later assembly such as `ftCo_800BD620` remained in that file.
Added `ftCo_CaptureKirby.h` declaring the new functions and temporary `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800BD1DC` / `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800BD39C` stack-layout structs.
Added `ftCommon_MotionVars.capturekirby` fields in `types.h`, including `pos_offset`, `x8`, `x10`, and `scale` mapped to offsets around `fp+2340` through `fp+236C`.
Registered `ftCo_CaptureKirby.c.o` in `obj_files.mk` and updated paths for several existing `ftCo_*` assembly files that were moved under `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/`.
Included `ftCo_CaptureKirby.h` from `ftCo_Init.c` so motion-state setup can see the CaptureKirby callback prototypes.
Updated many `ftKb_Init.h` prototypes from generic `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*`, including Kirby/Yoshi CaptureKirby-related declarations shown in the diff excerpt.
Added an `HSD_ASSERT(824, scale)` check to `HSD_JObjGetScale` in `jobj.h`, matching the CaptureKirby use of scale pointers.
Made a small formatting/control-flow cleanup in `DVDConvertPathToEntrynum` by bracing an empty `for` loop body.
smart_moves: Kept partial routines behind `ASM void` plus `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` C bodies and retained inline assembly fallbacks under the matching path, allowing readable progress without claiming full matches for `ftCo_800BD1DC` and `ftCo_800BD39C`.

Split recurring assembly patterns into local inline helpers (`inlineA0`, `inlineB0`, `inlineB1`) to isolate initialization, JObj scale updates, and position clamping logic while preserving the original stack/register-sensitive routines.
Introduced named motion-variable fields for CaptureKirby instead of continuing to access raw `fp+2340` offsets, improving future readability and making later matching work less offset-driven.
Moved exact small functions first: the squared-distance helper `ftCo_800BD19C` and the four empty action callbacks matched 100%, reducing assembly surface area safely.
Promoted constants `ftCo_804D8BA8`, `ftCo_804D8BAC`, and `ftCo_804D8BB0` into C near their use, mirroring the assembly constants for 0.0f, 1.0f, and `S32_TO_F32` conversion behavior.
Adjusted build-file ordering by inserting the new C object before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`, consistent with peeling C-matched functions out of a larger assembly translation unit.
decomp_lessons: For partial matches, preserve the original assembly as a guarded fallback and let the readable C path live under WIP/non-MUST_MATCH until function scores reach full match quality.
When extracting a subset from a large asm file, also move or expose nearby small data symbols/constants needed by the new C file; otherwise sdata/sdata2 ordering and references can drift.

Naming a union member like `mv.co.capturekirby` early is useful even when many subfields remain provisional (`x8`, `x10`, `x1C`, etc.), because it localizes future renaming to one struct instead of many raw offsets.
JObj helper assertions can affect matching: `HSD_JObjGetScale` needed an assertion for the `scale` argument, matching callsite behavior that asserted both `jobj` and `scale` in the assembly.

Callback signatures should use fighter-specific object typedefs (`Fighter_GObj*`) when the implementation immediately accesses fighter data; broad prototype cleanup may be required in character headers before new common-fighter C compiles cleanly.

Empty motion callbacks are low-risk wins and should be removed from asm early, but nearby accessory callbacks can still be complex due to stack temporaries, vector math, JObj scale writes, and matrix-dirty side effects.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_CaptureKirby_*` names are used for the common fighter action callbacks: `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Unidentified functions retained address-style names such as `ftCo_800BD19C`, `ftCo_800BD1DC`, and `ftCo_800BD39C`.
The new header guard used the address-based form `GALE01_0BD19C`.
Temporary stack structs followed m2c naming, e.g. `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800BD1DC` and `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800BD39C`.
Partially understood motion-variable fields used descriptive names where clear (`pos_offset`, `scale`) and offset-like placeholders where unclear (`x8`, `x10`, `x1C`, `x20`, `x24`, `x28`).
Kirby special-N helper declarations use the existing `ftKb_SpecialN_80xxxxxx` address-style convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `ASM void` function declarations for partially matched routines while embedding both C and assembly implementations under preprocessor guards.
Kept the original no-fralloc assembly bodies for `ftCo_800BD1DC` and `ftCo_800BD39C` under the fallback branch, with `#pragma peephole on` after the inline assembly blocks.
Modeled vector math with `SQ`, `ABS`, `sqrtf__Ff`, `Vec2`, and `Vec3` to correspond to repeated `fmuls/fadds/fsubs` patterns in the assembly.
Used ternary sign selection in clamping logic (`pos->x < 0 ? -1 : +1`) to model integer-to-float sign conversion patterns involving `xoris`, `lfd`, and `S32_TO_F32`.
Mapped assembly offsets `0x2340`, `0x2344`, `0x2348`, `0x234C`, `0x2350`, `0x2354`, and `0x236C` into the new `capturekirby` motion-variable struct.
Preserved accessory callback setup by assigning `fp->cb.x21B0_callback_Accessory1 = ftCo_800BD39C` in the C version of `ftCo_800BD1DC`.
Left partial matching evidence explicit in the PR report: `ftCo_800BD39C` scored 78.83% and `ftCo_800BD1DC` scored 88.93%, while several smaller functions were 100%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or submitted reviews were present in the PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `ftCo_800BD1DC` or `ftCo_800BD39C`.
Search for renames of `mv.co.capturekirby.x8` and `mv.co.capturekirby.x10` to determine their final semantic names.
Check whether the temporary `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800BD1DC` and `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800BD39C` structs were removed or replaced by real local-variable layouts.
Look for subsequent cleanup of `ftKb_Init.h` after the mass `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*` prototype conversion.
Audit later `ft_08A4.s` extraction PRs for reuse of the guarded partial-C plus asm-fallback pattern.
